required_skills: Astrodynamics and orbital mechanics including Keplerian motion, perturbation theory, and orbit propagation
Mission trajectory design for LEO, MEO, GEO, HEO, lunar, and interplanetary missions
Delta-V budget estimation and propulsion system sizing for orbit insertion, transfers, and station-keeping
Launch window analysis and launch vehicle selection trade studies
Coverage and revisit analysis for Earth observation, communications, and surveillance missions
Conjunction assessment and space debris avoidance manoeuvre planning
Ground station contact analysis and link budget support for mission operations planning
Spacecraft lifetime and end-of-life disposal orbit design per IADC debris mitigation guidelines
Monte Carlo dispersion analysis and statistical navigation error assessment
Model-based systems engineering (MBSE) for mission architecture definition and concept of operations
preferred_skills: Experience with low-energy transfer trajectories including Lagrange point orbits and ballistic lunar transfers
Knowledge of electric propulsion trajectory optimization using indirect or direct collocation methods
Familiarity with planetary entry, descent, and landing (EDL) trajectory design
Experience with constellation design and Walker delta pattern optimization for global coverage
Knowledge of autonomous onboard navigation and orbit determination techniques
Familiarity with NASA GMAT, JPL Monte, or GPOPS-II for trajectory optimization
Experience with cislunar mission architecture and Gateway or Artemis programme mission concepts
responsibilities: Design and optimise mission trajectories for spacecraft across LEO, GEO, lunar, and interplanetary target orbits using STK, GMAT, and Copernicus trajectory design tools
Develop and maintain delta-V budgets accounting for launch dispersion, orbit insertion errors, station-keeping, momentum management, and disposal across the full mission lifecycle
Perform launch window analysis by computing launch periods, C3 energy requirements, and planetary alignment constraints for time-critical and propulsion-limited missions
Conduct spacecraft coverage, revisit, and access analysis to verify that mission constellation architectures meet ground resolution, temporal sampling, and link availability requirements
Execute Monte Carlo trajectory dispersion analyses to quantify navigation uncertainties, assess manoeuvre execution errors, and define statistical delta-V margin requirements
Develop conjunction assessment procedures and design collision avoidance manoeuvres to ensure spacecraft safety in congested orbital regimes per space traffic management protocols
Define end-of-life deorbit and graveyard orbit disposal strategies compliant with IADC 25-year rule and national space debris mitigation guidelines
Collaborate with GNC, propulsion, and operations teams to translate mission design parameters into flight dynamics operational products including manoeuvre plans and ephemeris files
Support mission concept studies and Phase A/B architecture trade studies by quantifying mission performance sensitivity to orbit altitude, inclination, and launch vehicle selection
Author mission design documentation including trajectory design reports, mission analysis data packages, and operations concept documents to support design review milestones
